title: Agent 007 Retry Handoff
description: Written by Agent 007 when retrying a stalled or transiently-failed pipeline step. Distinct from normal pipeline handoffs — uses 007-retry-{N}.json naming and is NOT parsed by the standard handoff pipeline. The daemon HandoffHandler must skip files matching 007-*.json.

type.description: Discriminator field. Always 'retry' — distinguishes from normal pipeline handoffs.

retry_count.description: Which retry attempt this is (1-3). Enforced by 007 — escalates to human after 3.

spec_id.description: The spec ID of the pipeline that failed (e.g., 'EBATT-CALC-001').

failed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the failure was detected by the auditor.

error_summary.description: One-line summary of the error. Extracted from pane output or handoff.

auditor_check.description: Which auditor check triggered 007. Only retry-safe checks appear here.

auditor_severity.description: Always 'critical' — 007 is only triggered on critical+ severity.

classification.description: Only transient and stall classifications reach retry. Deterministic/configuration/unknown always escalate.

summary.description: 007's diagnosis of what went wrong and why retry is appropriate.

evidence.description: Evidence lines supporting the diagnosis. Pane output excerpts, timestamps, file contents.

instructions.description: What the retried agent should do differently. 007 provides context about the failure so the agent can adjust its approach.

target_agent.description: Same as failed_agent — retries always go back to the same agent.

original_handoff_path.description: Path to the last handoff file for this spec. Null if no handoff existed (e.g., agent stalled before writing one).
